name: Chicken & Cashews
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate chicken breasts for 30 minutes in salt, pepper, cornstarch, sherry, and egg.
2. Heat a wok until hot and dry.
3. Add oil to the wok. When it just begins to smoke, add chicken, green pepper, and water chestnuts, stirring for 1-2 minutes.
4. Drain through a colander or sieve, reserving 2-3 tablespoons of oil.
5. Return the reserved oil to the wok.
6. Add Imperial Sauce and hoisin sauce. Cook for 1-2 minutes, or until slightly thickened.
7. Add nuts, chicken, and vegetables. Stir everything together until well coated with the sauce.
8. Serve. Note: Hoisin sauce is pre-packaged and found in Chinese specialty stores. It is made from soybeans, flour, sugar, garlic, and chili peppers, giving it a reddish-brown color and creamy, sweet flavor. If too thick to spread, thin with some sesame oil.
